_source: National Tax Agency (国税庁) — Japan DTA network as of 2024
_notes: Japan has DTAs (租税条約) with ~80 countries. Key provisions relevant to FIRE: dividend/interest withholding rates, pension taxation, capital gains, and tie-breaker residency rules.

United States.pension_note: US Social Security taxable only in country of residence. If Japan-resident, taxed in Japan (not US). Foreign pension income deduction (公的年金等控除) applies.
key_notes: US Social Security benefits are taxable only in Japan if you are a Japan tax resident.
401k/IRA withdrawals: Japan taxes as miscellaneous income. No foreign pension deduction (not a government pension).
FATCA reporting required for US persons with Japanese financial accounts.
Exit tax (国外転出時課税) applies on unrealised gains >¥100M when leaving Japan.

United Kingdom.pension_note: UK State Pension taxable in Japan if Japan-resident. Foreign pension deduction applies.
key_notes: UK State Pension taxed only in Japan if Japan tax resident.
UK private/occupational pension: taxable in Japan as miscellaneous income.
ISA gains: Japan does not recognise ISA tax-free status — gains taxed in Japan.
Exit tax applies on departure from Japan with unrealised gains >¥100M.

Australia.pension_note: Australian Age Pension: taxable in Japan. Superannuation distributions: taxable in Japan (not covered by DTA as government pension).
key_notes: Superannuation is not a government pension — distributions are taxed in Japan as miscellaneous income.
Australian dividends: 10% WHT under treaty (vs 30% default).
Super contributions made while Japan-resident are not tax-deductible in Japan.

Canada.pension_note: CPP/OAS: taxable only in Japan if Japan-resident.
key_notes: CPP and OAS benefits taxed in Japan (not Canada) if Japan tax resident.
RRSP/RRIF withdrawals: Japan taxes as miscellaneous income.
TFSA gains: Japan does not recognise TFSA tax shelter — gains taxable in Japan.

Germany.pension_note: German statutory pension (gesetzliche Rentenversicherung): taxable in Japan.
key_notes: German pension taxed in Japan if Japan-resident.
Riester/Rürup pensions: no deduction in Japan; distributions taxed as income.

France.pension_note: French state pension (retraite de base): taxable in Japan.
key_notes: French pension taxed in Japan if Japan-resident.
PEA (Plan d'Épargne en Actions): Japan does not recognise tax-free status.

South Korea.pension_note: Korean National Pension (국민연금): taxable in Japan.
key_notes: Korean National Pension taxed in Japan if Japan-resident.
ISA/tax-free accounts in Korea: Japan does not recognise exemptions.

China.pension_note: No totalization agreement — pension contributions may be doubled.
key_notes: No totalization — working in both countries may require contributions to both pension systems simultaneously.
China-sourced income: report on Japan global income tax return.

Netherlands.pension_note: AOW (Dutch state pension): taxable in Japan.
key_notes: Dutch AOW pension taxed in Japan if Japan-resident.
Box 3 wealth tax: Japan residents are not subject to Dutch Box 3 but must report worldwide assets.

Switzerland.pension_note: Swiss AHV/AVS pension: taxable in Japan.
key_notes: Swiss AHV/AVS state pension taxed in Japan.
Pillar 3a: distributions taxed in Japan as miscellaneous income.

India.pension_note: EPS (Employees' Pension Scheme): limited treaty coverage.
key_notes: India–Japan totalization entered force 2016.
NPS (National Pension System): Japan may tax distributions as income.

exit_tax.applies_to: Unrealised gains on financial assets (stocks, bonds, investment trusts, not real estate) held by individuals with assets ≥¥100M who have lived in Japan ≥5 of the past 10 years.
exit_tax.note: 国外転出時課税 — Exit Tax. Triggered when leaving Japan permanently or temporarily (>5 years). Covered securities include NISA holdings (which lose their tax-free status on exit). Seek professional advice before emigrating with large NISA balance.

nhi_eligibility.ineligible_note: Short-stay visas (<3 months) and some special statuses may not qualify for NHI. Employer-sponsored shakai hoken (社会保険) provides separate coverage.

general_notes.global_income_reporting: Japan taxes residents on worldwide income. All foreign pensions, dividends, capital gains, and interest must be reported on the Japanese tax return (確定申告) regardless of where accounts are held.

residency_status_tax_impact.non_permanent_resident: Non-permanent residents (非永住者) — those who have been resident in Japan for fewer than 5 of the past 10 years and do not intend to stay — are taxed only on Japan-source income and foreign-source income remitted to Japan. This can significantly reduce the Japanese tax burden on foreign investment income.
residency_status_tax_impact.permanent_resident: Permanent residents and those who have lived in Japan ≥5 of 10 years are taxed on worldwide income regardless of remittance.
